title: The Body in the Library
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A retired schoolteacher turned amateur sleuth investigates when a body is found in the reference section of the local library with a Shakespeare quote pinned to its chest.
long_description:
When local historian Richard Caldwell is found dead in the reference section of Meadowfield Library with a line from Shakespeare pinned to his chest, retired schoolteacher Margaret Hartley knows she cannot look away. The victim was a quiet man who spent his days researching the town's history. Now someone has silenced him forever.

Inspector Reginald Cole of Scotland Yard arrives to lead the investigation, but his methodical approach misses the subtle clues hidden in the stacks. Margaret notices things others overlook: a book returned to the wrong shelf, underlined passages in old volumes, a photograph tucked inside a ledger.

As she follows the trail, she uncovers a sixty-year-old secret involving the library's revered founder, Sir Alistair Whitmore. A child died on his estate in 1957. The death was covered up by the town's most respected men. And now two more people are dead.

Margaret must piece together the truth from fragments of old records and reluctant witnesses before the killer silences her too. In a classic whodunit tradition, she gathers the suspects in the library's meeting room and reveals the killer's identity in a dramatic confrontation.

The Body in the Library is a literary cozy mystery about justice, memory, and the stories that libraries hold.
main_characters: Margaret Hartley — Retired schoolteacher and amateur sleuth
Inspector Reginald Cole — Scotland Yard detective
Eleanor Bane — The librarian, keeper of secrets
Richard Caldwell — The victim, local historian
Claire Caldwell — The victim's estranged daughter
Dr. Francis Pike — Antiquarian book dealer
Thomas Webb — Historical society president and killer
Sir Alistair Whitmore — Deceased philanthropist with a hidden past
